title: Support Ops Correlation Playbook
description: Correlate staff, calls, ASA, abandon, and survey scores weekly. Publish conclusions before metrics bite.

mindset.principle: Correlation is not causation, but it beats hallway blame. Write what the matrix says.

mistakes.label: Ding a team on one KPI without weekly context
mistakes.cost: Techs demand proof; trust in metrics dies

mistakes.label: Track CSAT only, ignore ASA and abandon
mistakes.cost: Miss the lever that moves answer scores

mistakes.label: QBR with slides and no ops correlation
mistakes.cost: Customer assumes you manage by narrative

correlation_insights.meaning: Staffing tracks volume; validate before cutting heads

correlation_insights.pattern: Very strong positive ASA vs abandon
correlation_insights.meaning: Speed and abandon move together; fix both or neither

correlation_insights.pattern: Moderate negative ASA vs answer survey
correlation_insights.meaning: Slower answer hurts on-time perception even when resolved

correlation_insights.pattern: Answer survey pulls other survey scores
correlation_insights.meaning: Overall is least dragged by answer alone; train holistically

decision_branches.situation: Leadership wants cause of CSAT move

steps.name: Refresh correlation matrix
steps.goal: Rolling window, not one bad week

agent_signal.use_when: Support ops and CSAT move together. Leadership dings on one KPI. QBR needs ops proof beyond surveys.
decision_criteria: Log weekly ops row before individual metric dings.
Publish correlation conclusions in plain language.
Pair ASA and abandon in staffing debates.
agent_signal.operator_context: Metric-heavy support orgs: matrix deflects blame wars and anchors QBR ops story.
agent_signal.not_for: Generic ops — fetch only when use_when matches; do not replace spine chain packs.
trigger_phrases: Support ops and CSAT move together. Leadership dings on one KPI. QBR needs ops proof beyond surveys.
Need playbook: Support Ops Correlation Playbook
